articles.headline: Chicago faces Dallas on 4-game slide
articles.description: Chicago Sky (4-10, 1-6 Eastern Conference) at Dallas Wings (9-6, 5-4 Western Conference)

articles.headline: Dream relish playoff-like intensity on road, hold off Fever
articles.description: In an early-season test between two title contenders, the Dream held off a late run by the Fever and won 108-101 Thursday night at Gainbridge Arena.

images.name: Angel Reese knocks down the dagger for the Dream
images.caption: Angel Reese knocks down the dagger for the Dream.

articles.headline: Reese has 21 points, 11 rebounds, and all Dream starters score 16-plus in 108-101 win over Fever
articles.description: — Angel Reese had 21 points and 11 rebounds for her 10th double-double of the season, Jordin Canada added 18 points, and the Atlanta Dream beat the Indiana Fever 108-101 on Thursday night.

images.name: Angel Reese knocks down the dagger for the Dream
images.caption: Angel Reese knocks down the dagger for the Dream.

articles.headline: Valkyries' Gabby Williams signing multiyear deal with Unrivaled
articles.description: Valkyries guard Gabby Williams is signing a multiyear deal with Unrivaled, the league announced Thursday.

articles.headline: How Caitlin Clark and the Indiana Fever have dialed in on defense
articles.description: After a 4-4 start, the Fever have settled into winning ways -- just in time for the toughest stretch of their season.

images.name: Indiana Fever celebrate [1296x729]
images.caption: After a 4-4 start to the season, the Indiana Fever have gone 5-1 since.

images.name: Caitlin Clark defense [1296x729]
images.caption: "I'm not a bad defender,” Clark told ESPN. “I've never been the best defender, and I understand that, but ... I can understand how I can help my teammates.

articles.headline: New York plays Washington, aims for 6th straight home win
articles.description: Washington Mystics (6-7, 5-4 Eastern Conference) at New York Liberty (11-4, 8-0 Eastern Conference)

articles.headline: Lynx's Miles sizzles in record half, finishes with 31 vs. Sparks
articles.description: Olivia Miles scored a WNBA rookie record 24 points in the first half and finished with a career-best 31 to lead the Lynx past the Sparks 99-83 on Wednesday night.

images.name: Olivia Miles [600x400]
images.credit: Photo by Tyler Ross/NBAE via Getty Images

images.name: Olivia Miles drops 31 points in Lynx's 99-83 win over Sparks
images.caption: Olivia Miles drops 31 points in Lynx's 99-83 win over Sparks

articles.headline: Wilson-led Aces clinch spot in WNBA Commissioner's Cup final
articles.description: The Aces will head to the WNBA Commissioner's Cup championship game for the third time thanks to an 86-76 win over the Mercury on Wednesday night.

images.name: A'ja Wilson goes off for 34 points in Aces' win
images.caption: A'ja Wilson goes off for 34 points in Aces' win

articles.headline: A'ja Wilson scores 33 points and Aces grab 18 steals in 86-76 win over Mercury
articles.description: — A’ja Wilson had 33 points and 11 rebounds Wednesday night, and the Las Vegas Aces beat the Phoenix Mercury 86-76 in a rematch of the 2025 WNBA Finals to clinch a spot in the Commissioner's Cup championship.

images.name: A’ja Wilson leads the Aces to victory with a 33-point double-double
images.caption: A’ja Wilson leads the Aces to victory with a 33-point double-double

articles.headline: Carelton scores 24 points on career-high 3-pointers as Fire beat Storm 94-89
articles.description: — Bridget Carleton had a career-high seven pointers and scored 24 points, Carla Leite added 20 points and the expansion Portland Fire beat the Seattle Storm 94-89 on Wednesday night in Commissioner’s Cup play.

images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Fire vs. Seattle Storm, 06/17/2026

article.headline: Howard scores 27, Miles adds 19, Lynx beat Storm 88-68 for seventh straight win
article.description: — Natasha Howard had a season-high 27 points on 12-of-16 shooting, Olivia Miles added 19 points, six assists and six rebounds, and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Seattle Storm 88-68 on Saturday for their seventh win in a row — the last six by double...
article.linkText: Howard scores 27, Miles adds 19, Lynx beat Storm 88-68 for seventh straight win

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Minnesota Lynx vs. Seattle Storm - Game Highlights) 2026/06/06 ESHEET

article.story: MINNEAPOLIS -- — Natasha Howard had a season-high 27 points on 12-of-16 shooting, Olivia Miles added 19 points, six assists and six rebounds, and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Seattle Storm 88-68 on Saturday for their seventh win in a row — the last six by double figures. Courtney Williams and Kayla McBride scored 11 points apiece for the Lynx (9-2). Howard and Nia Coffey each had three of Minnesota's season-high-tying 13 steals. The Storm (3-9), who have lost five in a row, committed a season-high 21 turnovers and shot just 35% (22 of 63) from the field. Coffey hit a 3-pointer with 3:09 remaining in the second quarter that gave Minnesota the lead for good at 39-38. Howard made two layups while Coffey and Maya Caldwell each hit a 3 in a 10-0 run that made it 55-43 less than three minutes into the third, and it was at least a nine-point game the rest of the way. Natisha Hiedeman and Jade Melbourne led Seattle with 14 points apiece. Flau'jae Johnson added 10 points. Dominique Malonga (concussion protocol) returned from an eight-game absence and finished with seven points in 14 minutes off the bench for the Storm. Up next Storm: Play Monday at Las Vegas. Lynx: Host Dallas on Tuesday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
